post.summary: Create the harbor's single `Registry` CR and block on the CR↔DB bridge.
post.description:
Lifecycle (mirrors `container_apps/create_crd.rs`):
1. resolve the org → slug (for the CR namespace `hf-<org-slug>`),
2. resolve the harbor → slug (for the CR name `<harbor-slug>-registry`),
3. validate the harbor belongs to the org,
4. validate the harbor slug / resulting CR name fits RFC1123 63 chars,
5. if the harbor already has a registry row, return it (one-per-harbor, idempotent),
6. build the `Registry` CR spec — `registry_base_domain` is templated from
   the console's configured ingress base domain (`registry_base_domain`
   config, falling back to `console_base_domain`), **never hardcoded**;
   `bucket_prefix` uses the CRD's `registry/` default,
7. create the CR (the console never touches K8s infra — the operator
   reconciles it),
8. `wait_for_registry_id` until the reconciler stamps the DB-row id; on
   timeout, DELETE the CR so we don't leave an orphan,
9. fetch the row from the DB and return it.

201.description: Registry provisioned (CR created + DB row bridged)

400.description: Harbor slug empty or would overflow RFC1123 63-char CR name limit

delete.summary:
Delete the harbor's `Registry` CR. The operator's finalizer cascades the
in-namespace children (Deployment/Service/route/ConfigMaps/creds Secret) and
`registries` row CASCADEs away with the harbor-scoped FK; the harbor's Ceph
RGW tenant + data are intentionally retained (the tenant outlives the registry).

oneOf.description:
Applies only if the principal carries attribute `key` with `value` among
its values. Sourced from the identity: curated claims (`email`,
`email_domain`, `client_id`) and the user's per-org RLS attributes
(`namespace::value`, grouped by namespace — e.g. `region == "occitanie"`).

Applies only if the request context carries attribute `key` with `value`
among its values — attributes projected from the call's own payload at
decision time (e.g. a secret's `secret_type`, or one of its `tag`s).

Condition.description:
An optional predicate that gates whether a grant applies, evaluated at
decision time against the principal's attributes (ABAC) or the request
context derived from the call's payload.

The tagged representation lets new context sources be added without
reshaping stored grants. Matching is membership-based: a key may carry
multiple values (a user holding `region::occitanie` and `region::paris`, or
a create request tagging several labels), and the condition holds when
`value` is among them.

ConditionView.description:
Generic Kubernetes-style condition surfaced to the console UI.

Mirrors the shape used on `OrgStorageStatus.conditions` and
`HarborStorageBindingStatus.conditions`. The CRD-side enums for
`type_` are flattened to plain strings (the variant names) so the
frontend can render unknown future condition types without a code
update.

ConnectorDescriptorResponse.description:
Static description of one opendata connector (slug + human title + JSON
Schema of the connector's params, used to drive the dynamic UI form).

params_schema.description: JSON Schema object describing the connector's `params` shape.

resource_version.description:
The Kubernetes resourceVersion of this object at read time.
Must be round-tripped verbatim as `resource_version` in PATCH requests
to enable optimistic concurrency control; the server returns 409 Conflict
when the live object has advanced past this version.

CopyDestinationConfig.description:
Copy pipeline destination configuration (S3 bucket).

Symmetric with [`CopyS3SourceConfig`]. Two variants are accepted:

* **`DataContainer`** (legacy) — a `DataContainerBucket` UUID from Postgres.
  The DataDock that owns the container is resolved automatically, and the
  shared-RGW path is attempted first with a fallback to the legacy per-DataDock
  Vault credentials.

* **`HarborBucket`** (ADR-0008) — an `HFBucket` CRD name under a Harbor UUID.
  The bucket must exist and be Ready. **No legacy fallback** — if the Harbor
  has no `HarborStorageBinding` pointing at a Ready `OrgStorage`, the request
  fails immediately with a clear error.

CopyS3SourceConfig.description:
S3 source configuration for S3-to-S3 copy.

Two variants are accepted:

* **`DataContainer`** (legacy) — a `DataContainerBucket` UUID from Postgres.
  Behaves identically to `FileSorterSourceConfig::DataContainer`.

* **`HarborBucket`** (ADR-0008) — an `HFBucket` CRD name under a Harbor UUID.

oneOf.description: Stamped with `source=ui`. Deleting from the UI is the normal path.

Stamped with `source=pipeline`. Deleting is legit (one-shot
imports, cleanup after a bad run) but should be explicit —
`name` carries whichever pipeline identifier the operator wrote.

Any ArgoCD/Flux/Helm marker detected. Deleting here is almost
always pointless — the GitOps reconciler will recreate the CRD
on its next sync.

reference.description:
Best-effort app / release identifier (ArgoCD app name, Helm
release name, Flux kustomization). `None` if the marker is a
bare `managed-by` label without a qualifying annotation.

oneOf.description:
No ownership markers at all — legacy CRD or created by a tool we
don't recognize.

CrdOwner.description:
Who "owns" a CRD from the console's point of view — derived purely
from metadata markers, never from the CRD's spec. Useful to explain to
the user what will happen when they delete it.

CrdStatusSnapshot.description:
Projection of a Hyperfluid CRD's `.status` subresource, suitable for
surfacing in list endpoints so the UI can render a live status badge
without having to call kube-apiserver itself.

- For `TrinoSchema`: `phase` + `message` are populated; drift fields
  stay empty.
- For `TrinoTable`: all four fields are meaningful — `missing_columns`
  and `extra_columns` come from the reconciler's drift detection.

extraColumns.description:
Columns present in Trino but not declared in the CRD spec. The
reconciler never drops these automatically. Empty for schemas.

missingColumns.description:
Columns declared in the CRD spec but absent from the actual Trino
table (subject to opt-in migration). Empty for schemas.

phase.description:
`Pending` / `Ready` / `Drifted` / `Failed` / `Terminating`.
Stringly-typed because `TrinoSchema` and `TrinoTable` don't share
the same phase enum (Drifted is Table-only).

parameters.description:
Concrete parameter values. Keys must match `AppTemplate.spec.parameters[*].name`.
Validation against the declared parameter schema happens in the operator.

slug.description:
DNS-1123 slug — also the CRD object name in the org namespace, and the
initial display name (override later via `PATCH .../app-instances/{slug}`).

description.description: Optional description (DB-only, not stored in CRD)

insecure.description:
Skip TLS certificate verification when probing the S3 endpoint.
Intended for development (self-signed certs). Defaults to false.
Ignored for `internal` source — credentials resolved from OrgStorage
go to a cluster-local plaintext RGW endpoint.

name.description: Must be a valid slug: lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens only; cannot start or end with a hyphen.

tags.description: User-defined tags (DB-only, not stored in CRD)

CreateConnectorIngestRequest.description:
Connector-based ingest pipeline request: run an opendata connector and load
the result into an Iceberg table in the Trino catalog of the destination
data container (`data_container_id`).

data_container_id.description:
Destination Iceberg data container UUID; its Trino catalog receives the
table the connector data is loaded into.

params.description:
Connector-specific parameters as a JSON object.
The shape is described by the connector's `params_schema` field from the
`/api/v1/opendata/connectors` endpoint.

schema_name.description: Trino schema name for the destination table.

table_name.description: Trino table name to create (full-refresh each run).

custom_domains.description:
Bring-your-own custom domains. Each FQDN's apex must have a `Verified`
`DomainVerification` for the operator to provision a route + cert; otherwise
the per-domain status sits in `PendingVerification` until the user completes
ownership verification.

file_mounts.description:
Files to mount into the container at startup. Each entry is either an
inline literal or a reference to a platform secret. Inline content is
hard-capped at 8 KiB per file and 32 KiB total across all entries —
file mounts are for small configs, not data.

image_pull_secrets.description: Names of secrets from the secret store to use as image pull secrets.

name.description: Must be a valid slug: lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens only; cannot start or end with a hyphen.

persistence.description:
Persistent volumes attached to the container. Each entry is reconciled
into a `PersistentVolumeClaim` owned by the `ContainerApp`. Capped at 8
entries. PVCs are always `ReadWriteOnce`; `size` and `storageClass` are
immutable after creation in v1 — volume expansion is not yet supported.

CreateContextEnricherPipelineRequest.description:
Context Enricher pipeline request: enrich Iceberg table with L0/L1 abstracts via LLM.
Reads content (L2) from a source table, writes enriched rows (content + L0 + L1) to a destination table.
Enables lineage: object storage → OCR → ice_raw → hippocampe → ice_enriched

CreateContextProviderBody.description: Request to create a context provider.

config.description: Configuration JSON based on provider type

CreateContextualRestrictionBody.description: Request to create a contextual restriction.

CreateCopyPipelineRequest.description: Copy pipeline request: Import API source → S3 destination

code_location_image.description:
Image of the user-code (code location) gRPC server. When omitted, the
operator-default image is used.

compute_log_bucket.description: Name of an existing object-storage bucket for Dagster compute logs.

description.description: Optional description (DB-only, not stored in the CRD).

name.description: Must be a valid slug: lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens only; cannot start or end with a hyphen.

postgres_ref.description:
Name of an existing `ManagedPostgreSQL` (in the Harbor namespace) to host
Dagster's run/event/schedule storage. When omitted, the operator
provisions a dedicated `ManagedPostgreSQL`.

sleep_mode.description: Sleep mode: scale webserver + daemon to 0 (schedules stop firing).

tags.description: User-defined tags (DB-only, not stored in the CRD).

extra_fqdns.description: Extra FQDNs this workstation may reach, on top of the profile.

inference_model.description:
Provider-qualified opencode model id the agent defaults to, e.g.
`deepseek/deepseek-chat` or `anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet`. When omitted,
the workstation entrypoint's curated default for the provider applies.

inference_provider.description:
Inference provider for the agent: `sapience` (in-cluster LLMaaS, default)
or `openrouter` (external). Omitted/empty falls back to `sapience`.

llm_providers.description:
Managed external LLM providers the agent may reach (openrouter,
anthropic, openai, gemini). Defaults to [openrouter] when omitted.

openrouter_key_secret_id.description:
Id of a Passe Partout secret of type `plaintext` holding the OpenRouter
API key (`sk-or-...`). Required when `inference_provider` is `openrouter`.

scm_secret_id.description:
Id of a Passe Partout secret of type `scm_credential` holding the git token.
Required for `github`; omit for an in-cluster `forgejo` workstation — the
operator mints a token from the owner's OIDC identity (ADR-0016 phase 2).

template_ref.description:
CRD name of a [`WorkstationTemplate`] to resolve as the workstation's
environment (ADR-0015). When set, the template is resolved at create time
and the resulting base image + setup script are embedded in the CR spec.
When absent, the workstation uses the platform default base, no extra setup.

template_version.description:
Template version to pin. When set alongside `template_ref`, the resolve
step checks that the template's version matches this value. When absent,
the current version is used and recorded.

apex.description:
Apex domain to verify ownership of, e.g. `acme.com`. Must be a lowercase
fully-qualified domain name; immutable after creation.

routing_rules.description: Routing rules: label -> list of destinations

CreateFileSorterRequest.description: FileSorter pipeline request with semantic groupings

CreateForgejoRunnerRequest.description:
Request body for creating a `ForgejoRunner` CR.

The forge coordinates (namespace, container-app label) are resolved
automatically from `harbor_id` via `resolve_forge`; the caller never
supplies them directly.

extra_egress_fqdns.description:
Extra FQDNs the runner's `CiliumNetworkPolicy` allows egress to,
beyond the mandatory set (in-cluster forge + console token endpoint).

labels.description:
Forgejo runner labels assigned during registration
(e.g. `["ubuntu-latest", "docker"]`).  Determines which `runs-on:`
tags the runner accepts.

name.description:
CR name — must be a valid DNS-1123 label:
lowercase alphanumeric characters and hyphens only, 1–63 chars,
cannot start or end with a hyphen.

replicas.description: Number of runner pod replicas. Defaults to 1.

runner_image.description:
Runner container image. Defaults to the pinned Forgejo runner release
(`DEFAULT_RUNNER_IMAGE`). Override to pin a different tag or use a
custom image compatible with the Forgejo runner binary CLI.

destination_data_container_id.description: Destination S3 DataDock to land the files into

scope_path.description:
Optional scope: a path in the org resource tree (exact, an ancestor
folder, or a `*`-suffixed wildcard), interpreted within the permission's
own resource family. Omit (or empty) for an org-wide grant. The resource
*type* is derived from `permission_id` — never sent — so a grant's type
can't drift from the permission it gates.

name.description:
Must be a valid RFC 1035 label: lowercase, starts with a letter, ends
with a letter or digit, only letters/digits/hyphens in between.

CreateIcebergTableMetadataRequest.description:
Request to create Iceberg table metadata.

The modern flow is structured: the client sends `columns`,
`medallion_tier`, and optional partitioning/sort/properties; the backend
generates the DDL, executes it, saves metadata, and materializes the
TrinoTable CRD from the same structured input.

`ddl_statement` is kept as an optional backward-compatibility field for
callers that still generate DDL client-side. When both are present,
structured fields win and `ddl_statement` is ignored.

backup_target_id.description:
BackupTarget to use for on-demand and scheduled backups. Required when
`backup_policy` is `automated`; ignored when `manual`.

database_name.description:
Name of the application database to create inside the PostgreSQL cluster.
Must be a valid PostgreSQL identifier: lowercase letters, digits, and underscores only; must start with a letter or underscore.

description.description: Optional description for this database instance

name.description: Must be a valid slug: lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens only; cannot start or end with a hyphen.

username.description:
Postgres role name. Lowercase letters, digits and underscores; must start
with a letter or underscore. The CRD `metadata.name` is derived by
replacing `_` with `-`, so the same identifier shows up in the UI, the
Postgres cluster, and Kubernetes.

max_model_len.description: Maximum model context length (optional, for generation models)

CreateOpenDataPipelineRequest.description: Open Data pipeline request: download open data CSV and load into Iceberg table

action.description:
Action pattern the guardrail governs: exact (`secret:read`) or `<type>:*`
wildcard (`secret:*`). The resource family is derived from its prefix.

requirements.description:
Conditions that must ALL hold for access; otherwise the guardrail denies.
Keys include the principal's `role`, RLS attributes, and request context.

resource_id.description:
Resource id pattern within the family: exact, ancestor folder, or a
`*`-suffixed wildcard (e.g. `prod/asia/*`).

CreatePageSplitterPipelineRequest.description: PageSplitter pipeline request: splits OCR'd documents by page markers

Copy: Import API source → S3 destination

GDrive: download files from GDrive and load into S3
OpenData: download open data CSV and load into Iceberg table

Managed PostgreSQL user whose credentials back the catalog. Must be a
read-only (`viewer`) user of a DBaaS cluster in the same organization
— the console derives the connection secret from the user's CRD
status, so arbitrary secrets can no longer be linked.

name.description: Trino catalog name.

Human-readable name for the robot; also used as the display name of the
underlying service account. Must be 3–64 characters.
The `<project>` path segment in `<org>/<harbor>/<project>/<image>`.

id.description:
Opencode session id (forwarded verbatim from the upstream response).
Named `id` so the frontend's `AgentSession` shape lines up with both
this create response and the normalized session list.

CreateTableClassificationBody.description: Request to create a table classification.

When true, adds a PageSplitter step after OCR to split documents by page markers.
The split pages are written to a table named `{table_name}_split_pages` in the same schema.

CreateWorkstationTemplateRequest.description: Request body for creating a workstation template.

baseImage.description:
Fully-qualified base image reference. Must be on the vetted-base
allow-list (`DEV_WORKSTATION_VETTED_BASE_IMAGES` env, plus the platform
default `registry.hyperfluid.cloud/ws-base/default:dev`).

icon.description: URL of the icon shown on the catalog card.

name.description:
Desired CRD name (ASCII, slugified by the server). When absent, the
server generates a name from `displayName`.

organizationId.description:
Organization the template is created in. Required for `org` and `user`
scopes.

script.description:
Raw, idempotent bash setup script. When non-empty, takes precedence over
the `tooling`-generated script.

tooling.description:
High-level tool declarations. Resolved into a setup script by the server
when `script` is absent or empty.

CustomDomainResponse.description: Combined spec + status for one `customDomains[]` entry, returned to the UI.

certificate_secret_name.description: Live TLS Secret backing the route (either user-provided or cert-manager-issued).

state.description:
Per-domain lifecycle from `status.customDomains[]`.
One of `pendingVerification`, `provisioning`, `ready`, `error`. `None` until the
operator has reconciled this entry.

target.description: CNAME target the user must point their DNS at. Surfaced once the route exists.

DagsterCrdSpecResponse.description:
The live `Dagster` CRD spec (source of truth in K8s), including fields the DB
does not cache (sleep_mode).

DataContainerOverview.description: A summary of a data container

internal_host.description:
internal_host and internal_port composes the address to reach the data dock from inside the cluster.
This is mainly used for introspection

port.description: This is i16 to match the sqlx type for port but this should never be negative (constraints are already in place in the db)

sts_role_arn.description:
IAM role ARN for STS AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity (required for Ceph RGW)
Format: arn:aws:iam:::role/<role-name>

token_issuer_endpoint.description:
token issuer endpoint is the url to get
the token related to the service account

DataDockExternalResponseData.description: External API response — strips infra details (namespaces) from user-facing endpoints.

deleted.description: Number of secrets deleted under the prefix.

DeletePreviewDownstream.description:
A downstream resource that will be affected by the delete.
Currently scoped to child tables for a schema preview; the shape is
generic so we can add pipelines / queries later without breaking the
contract.

detail.description:
Optional hint surfaced next to the name (e.g. owner of the child
table, next run time for a pipeline).

kind.description: `trino_table` / `pipeline` — a short tag the UI switches on.

crd_present.description:
`true` when a matching Hyperfluid CRD was found. If `false`, the
delete will go straight to DDL and no cascade concerns apply.

downstream.description:
Resources that will also be affected. Empty for table deletes
today; populated for schema deletes with child-table count.

fqn.description:
Fully-qualified name of the target (`catalog.schema` or
`catalog.schema.table`).

short_name.description:
Canonical unqualified name the user types in the confirm box.
Emitted by the server (instead of letting the client `split(".")`
the `fqn`) so identifiers with embedded dots — Trino allows
`"schema.with.dots"` when quoted — don't round-trip to a garbled
token the user can't physically type.

warnings.description:
Pre-rendered, user-facing warnings. The UI can render them
directly; severity controls the color.

DestinationConfig.description:
Configuration for a FileRouter destination
Allows routing to different Data Docks with specific prefixes

bucket_id.description: UUID of the bucket Data Container within the destination data dock

data_dock_id.description: UUID of the destination Data Dock (S3)

prefix.description: Prefix path within the destination bucket (e.g., "invoices/processed/")

DevWorkstationMetrics.description:
Live resource usage for a workstation pod. Every field is independently
nullable: a `null` means "couldn't be measured right now" (metrics-server
unavailable, exec failed, no limit set), not zero.

cpu_limit_millicores.description: Sum of container CPU limits, in millicores. `null` when no limit is set.

cpu_millicores.description:
CPU currently used, in millicores. From the metrics API when available,
otherwise sampled from the container cgroup. `null` only when both fail.

disk_total_bytes.description: Total bytes of the `/workspace` volume. `null` when the `df` exec failed.

disk_used_bytes.description: Bytes used on the `/workspace` volume. `null` when the `df` exec failed.

memory_bytes.description:
Memory currently used, in bytes. From the metrics API when available,
otherwise read from the container cgroup. `null` only when both fail.

memory_limit_bytes.description: Sum of container memory limits, in bytes. `null` when no limit is set.

preview_urls.description: Dev-server previews exposed as per-port subdomains.

appsUsing.description:
ContainerApps in the same org that have a `customDomain` falling under this apex.
Always empty in list responses to avoid N+1 K8s calls — populated on detail only.

expectedTxtRecord.description:
Pre-computed DNS record name where the user must place the TXT challenge,
e.g. `_hyperfluid-challenge.acme.com`.

expectedTxtValue.description:
Pre-computed TXT value the user must publish, e.g.
`hyperfluid-domain-verification=ABCD1234...`.

lastCheckError.description: Error from the most recent failed poll. Cleared on success.

lastCheckedAt.description: Timestamp of the most recent poll attempt (regardless of outcome).

verifiedAt.description: Timestamp at which ownership was successfully verified.

DynamicLabel.description:
Dynamic label configuration with format string and placeholders.
The format string contains placeholders like {year}, {date}, {name} that
get replaced with extracted values.

placeholders.description: Placeholders to extract from the document

DynamicLabelPlaceholder.description:
Placeholder definition for dynamic labels.
Used to extract variable parts from documents (dates, names, etc.)

rules.description: Validation rules for the extracted value

source.description: Instructions for the LLM on how to extract this value from the document

Effect.description:
Whether a grant confers or withdraws access. Deny is evaluated with strict
precedence: any matching deny overrides every allow (see the Rego).

EnableSqlEngineRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /api/v1/harbors/{harbor_id}/sql-engine/enable`.

Creates a TrinoInternal DataDock CRD and (optionally) a demo Iceberg
catalog + backing HFBucket in one declarative shot. Returns immediately;
the operator reconciles all three CRDs in dependency order without any
synchronous wait from the console.

bucket_name.description:
Name of the HFBucket to create/reuse for the Iceberg warehouse
(only used when `seed_demo_catalog = true`). Defaults to
`"demo-iceberg"`.

catalog_name.description:
Name of the Iceberg catalog to create (only used when
`seed_demo_catalog = true`). Defaults to `"demo_iceberg"`.

name.description: Display name for the Trino cluster.

seed_demo_catalog.description:
When true, also creates a demo HFBucket and an Iceberg TrinoCatalog
with medallion scaffold enabled. Both are idempotent (409 = already
exists is treated as success). Defaults to false.

slug.description:
K8s-safe slug (lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens). Used as the DataDock
CRD name and as the `data_dock_ref` in the TrinoCatalog.

worker_replicas.description: Number of Trino worker replicas. Clamped to ≥1. Defaults to 2.

EnableSqlEngineResponse.description:
Response for `POST /api/v1/harbors/{harbor_id}/sql-engine/enable`.

Returned immediately after the CRDs are applied; the operator reconciles
asynchronously. `catalog_name` is `null` when `seed_demo_catalog` was
`false`.

catalog_name.description:
Name of the Iceberg catalog CRD that was created, or `null` when
`seed_demo_catalog` was `false`.

data_dock_slug.description: Slug of the DataDock CRD that was created.

ExposedTableRef.description: A `(schema, table)` pair in a catalog's exposure allowlist.

ExposureMode.description:
How a Trino catalog is surfaced through Bifrost and the unified catalog.

No `catalog_exposures` row for a catalog is equivalent to [`ExposureMode::All`]
— the historical "everything is visible" behavior — so only catalogs that
opt into curation pay the lookup.

content.description: UTF-8 content. Empty for binary, too-large, missing, or non-regular files.

editable.description:
True when the file may be edited in-browser (regular + UTF-8 + within the
editable size cap).

hash.description:
sha256 of the on-disk bytes — the autosave precondition token. Empty when
the file does not exist.

reason.description:
Why the file is view-only / unavailable when `editable` is false:
`missing` | `directory` | `binary` | `too_large`. `None` when editable.

size.description: File size in bytes (0 when missing).

mode.description:
Octal file mode as a string (e.g. `"0644"`, `"0400"`). Optional — the
platform defaults to `0644` for inline files and `0400` for secret-backed
files.

mount_path.description:
Absolute filesystem path inside the container, e.g.
`/etc/nginx/conf.d/site.conf`. Must start with `/`.

FileMountResponse.description: A single file-mount entry returned as part of the CRD GET response.

mode.description:
Octal file mode, e.g. `"0644"`. `None` means the platform default will
be used (`0644` for inline, `0400` for secret-backed).

mount_path.description: Absolute filesystem path inside the container.

oneOf.description:
Literal bytes stored inline in the spec. Per-file cap 8 KiB; total
cap across all file mounts on the app is 32 KiB.

Reference to a platform secret by name. Resolved at reconcile time and
dropped at `mount_path` inside the container.

FileMountSourceInput.description:
Source for a file mount, as accepted by the public API. Discriminated by
`type`. Maps to `FileMountSource` on the CRD side (which is flat with
optional per-variant fields, since the CRD schema generator can't yet
express tagged enums).

oneOf.description: Content is stored inline in the spec.

content.description:
Literal file content. Empty string is valid (e.g. an intentionally
empty config file).

oneOf.description: Content comes from a platform secret manager entry.

secret_name.description: Name of the secret in the control-plane secret store.

FileMountSourceResponse.description:
Source for a file mount entry in the GET response.

Mirrors `FileMountSource` in the CRD model but uses a strongly-typed
discriminated-union shape so the UI can branch on `type` without string
matching on optional fields.

FileSorterDestinationConfig.description: FileSorter destination configuration (S3 bucket for sorted files)

data_container_id.description: Data Container ID for the destination bucket (null = use source bucket)

FileSorter output configuration - combines 3 steps:
HephaistosPdfeed -> Labelize -> CategoryRouterMetadataAware

dc_iceberg_id.description: UUID of the Iceberg catalog Data Container

dd_trino_int.description: UUID of the Trino Data Dock for metadata storage

destination_bucket_id.description: UUID of the destination bucket Data Container for sorted files

destination_s3_dd.description: UUID of the destination S3 Data Dock for sorted files

labels_yaml.description:
Labels for document classification in YAML format.
Supports both static and dynamic labels with placeholders.

Example:
```yaml
- id: facture_eau
  type: static
  description: "Water utility invoices"
- id: travaux
  type: dynamic
  description: "Construction work documents"
  format: "TRAVAUX/{year}/{date}"
  placeholders:
    - id: year
      source: "Extract the year"
      format: "YYYY"
```

model_api_key.description: API key for OCR and classification model

model_base_url.description: Base URL for the model API (e.g., vLLM endpoint). Defaults to Mistral API when not set.

source_file_connection_id.description: Source S3 connection ID for router (references input.dd_source)

unknown_placeholder_value.description: Value to use when a placeholder cannot be extracted (default: "unknown")

default_category.description: Default category if no label matches

source_prefix_regex.description: Regex to extract prefix from source path (single capture group)

oneOf.description: Legacy path: resolve via DataContainerBucket UUID.

FileSorterSourceConfig.description:
FileSorter source configuration (S3 bucket for unsorted files).

Two variants are accepted:

* **`DataContainer`** (legacy) — a `DataContainerBucket` UUID from Postgres.
  The DataDock that owns the container is resolved automatically, and the
  shared-RGW path is attempted first with a fallback to the legacy per-DataDock
  Vault credentials.

* **`HarborBucket`** (ADR-0008) — an `HFBucket` CRD name under a Harbor UUID.
  The bucket must exist and be Ready in the cluster. **No legacy fallback** —
  if the Harbor has no `HarborStorageBinding` pointing at a Ready `OrgStorage`,
  the request fails immediately with a clear error.

Grant.description:

Label.description:
Full label definition supporting both static and dynamic types.
This is the primary type used for document classification labels.

Example YAML:
```yaml
# Static label
- id: facture_eau
  type: static
  description: "Water utility invoices"

# Dynamic label with placeholders
- id: travaux
  type: dynamic
  description: "Construction work documents"
  format: "TRAVAUX/{year}/{date}-{work_name}"
  placeholders:
    - id: year
      source: "Extract the year from the document"
      format: "YYYY"
      rules: ["Must be 4 digits"]
```

One entry in the orphaned-pools inventory exposed to the console UI.

"Orphaned" = a CephObjectStore (and its underlying RADOS pools) that

carries no `data-dock-id` label at all. Sourced from the ConfigMap written
by the operator's periodic scan (ADR-0008 Phase 1; see
`hf_bin_operator/.../ceph/orphaned_pools.rs`).

`size_bytes` is forward-looking: today's operator scan does not collect
pool sizes (would require a Ceph admin connection inside the controller),
so the field is `None`. Keeping it on the wire format means the frontend
(#2224) can be wired against the final shape without a second round-trip
when the operator starts gathering it.

PatchAppInstanceRequestBody.description:
PATCH body for updating an app instance's DB-only metadata. Does NOT touch
the CRD — use `PATCH .../crd` for spec changes (parameters).

description.description: Optional free-text description. Pass `null` to clear an existing description.

tags.description: Replacement tag list. Pass an empty array to clear all tags.

clear_health_check.description:
When `true`, removes any existing health check from the spec. Mutually
exclusive with `health_check_path` / `health_check_port`: sending both
returns a 400 Bad Request.

custom_domains.description:
Custom domains to expose the app on. PATCH semantics: `null` (omit)
leaves existing entries alone; an empty array clears them; a non-empty
array replaces the full list.

file_mounts.description:
Files to mount into the container. PATCH semantics: `null` (omit) leaves
existing entries alone; an empty array clears them; a non-empty array
replaces the full list. Subject to the same per-file and total inline
size caps as `POST`.

image_pull_secrets.description: Names of secrets from the secret store to use as image pull secrets. Replaces existing pull secrets when set.

persistence.description:
Persistent volumes attached to the container. PATCH semantics: `null`
(omit) leaves existing entries alone; an empty array removes every
entry from the spec; a non-empty array replaces the full list.

Note: removing an entry does NOT delete the underlying PVC. The
operator surfaces `PersistenceReady=False` (`OrphanPersistenceVolumes`)
listing the now-unreferenced PVCs and leaves them intact so data is
never lost by accident. The only way to release the volumes is to
delete the `ContainerApp` itself, at which point owner-reference GC
releases the underlying PVs per the StorageClass `reclaimPolicy`.

`size` and `storageClass` are immutable per entry —
the operator surfaces `PersistenceReady=False` (`ImmutablePersistenceChanged`)
and refuses to apply the workload until the change is reverted. Adding
or removing entries is allowed (subject to the orphan behavior above).

resource_version.description:
If set, the patch will be rejected with 409 Conflict when the live object's
resourceVersion differs. Recommended for any UI-driven edit to prevent
silent last-write-wins on concurrent modifications.

ProjectMemberRole.description:
Role of a **named user** member within a registry project, expressed as the
set of grants they hold at the project scope path.

## Design note — owner vs editor

ADR-0014 v1 defined `viewer | editor | owner`. In the grants-based
implementation, `owner` is indistinguishable from `editor`: both hold
`registry:pull + registry:push + registry:delete` at the project scope.
The management API therefore exposes only `viewer | editor`. If
per-project-owner semantics (e.g. a `registry:manage` permission for
adding/removing members) are needed in the future, add a new permission key
rather than re-introducing a members table or a third role variant.

Ref.description:
A reference to a file in a storage bucket
This reference is created upon scanning a bucket and is used to track the
status of the file through the pipeline.

labels.description:
ARN-style metadata accumulated per step for this ref. Append-only.
Format `hf:pipelines:{kind}[:{qualifier}]::{value}` — see
`crate::models::labels` for builders. Used by retriable steps to skip
already-completed work and by routing steps to record produced paths.

locked_by.description:
The ID of the pipeline run that locked the reference
This is used to track the progress of the pipeline run
If the pipeline run is unhealthy (missed a heartbeat), the reference
will be unlocked and the pipeline run will be considered failed.

sources.description:

`"shared_rgw"`. Duplicates are kept so callers can see one entry per
connection that was patched.

RegistryResponse.description:
The `registries` row returned by both the internal (operator) and external
(UI) registry routes. Defined once here to avoid the `RegistryResponse` /
`RegistryCrdResponse` split that previously existed across the bridge and
external create handlers — both structs had identical fields and identical
`From<domain::Registry>` impls.

`is_ready` and `external_endpoint` are operator-synced status fields
(persisted via the internal `update_registry_status` endpoint). External
read handlers (list, get) serve these from the DB — never from the K8s API
server (CLAUDE.md operator architecture rule).

Referenced in both `InternalRegistriesApi` and `ExternalRegistriesApi`
OpenAPI component schemas; utoipa deduplicates by schema name so the single
`RegistryResponse` definition is emitted once in each generated spec.

external_endpoint.description:
The external registry host once the reconciler reconciles the
Ingress/HTTPRoute (`registry.<base-domain>`). `None` until ready.

harbor_id.description: Harbor that owns this registry (one-per-harbor, ADR-0014 §Scoping).

RegistryRole.description:
A member's role within the registry org (ADR-0014 frozen contract).

The DB stores this as the `registry_role` Postgres enum; the Rust mapping
follows the prevailing `secret_type` pattern (`sqlx::Type` +
`#[sqlx(type_name = ...)]`).

**Role → registry-scope mapping (v1)** — see [`RegistryRole::scopes`]:
`viewer` → `pull`; `editor`/`owner` → `pull,push,delete`. `editor` ≡ `owner`
in v1, kept distinct in the enum for later tightening (no data migration when
they diverge). `manage` is a console-API capability, NOT a registry scope.

RegistryTokenResponse.description: Docker token-auth success body (returned by the real impl in #2294).

RegistryUsageResponse.description:
The per-harbor registry usage / quota read model (ADR-0014 #2303).

Returns the **exact per-harbor quota limit** (from the Registry CR
`spec.storage_quota_gb`; `null` ⇒ inherits the org `OrgStorage` quota), plus
repository / tag **counts** and a **best-effort** size sum derived from the
manifests the caller can pull. **Byte-level per-project usage accounting is
DEFERRED to #2300** (push-time blob accounting in the broker); until then
`byte_usage_pending = true` flags every size field as approximate and not
authoritative — the UI (#2307) must label it as such.

approximate_size_bytes.description:
Best-effort total size in bytes (`Σ config + layers` over visible tags).
**Approximate** — see `byte_usage_pending`.

byte_usage_pending.description:
`true` in v1: byte-level usage is best-effort (summed from manifests the
caller can see, blobs double-counted across projects à la Harbor) and
**authoritative per-project byte accounting is pending #2300**. The UI
must surface size as approximate while this is `true`.

quota_limit_gb.description:
Per-org storage quota in GB (exact, via the Ceph tenant `max_size`).
`null` means the registry inherits the org's `OrgStorage` quota (the
effective limit is resolved by the operator and not on the CR spec).

repository_count.description: Total repositories the caller can see (all projects, gated by org role).

name.description:
New tmux session name. Must match the same charset as creation
(`[A-Za-z0-9_-]`, max 64); the server re-sanitizes regardless.

ResourceRequirementsSpec.description:
Resource requests and limits for the container.

`limits.cpu` is intentionally omitted from most defaults: an unset CPU limit
avoids CPU throttling (the kernel CFS scheduler throttles containers that
hit their quota even when the node has spare capacity). Set it only when you
need hard isolation guarantees.

ResourceSource.description:
Whether a schema / table was declared via a Hyperfluid CRD
(`TrinoSchema`, `TrinoTable`) or materialized ad-hoc through Trino DDL.

Lives here (domain layer) rather than in the HTTP DTO layer because
both domain models and HTTP responses carry this label.

ResourceSpec.description: CPU / memory quantity for a single resource axis (e.g. "500m", "256Mi").

ResourceTier.description:
Predefined resource tiers for container apps.
Memory limits are always equal to memory requests.

name.description:
Human-readable name to preserve on the new SA. Required to avoid a
round-trip lookup (the old SA's name is not guaranteed to survive the
delete-recreate cycle in all timing windows).

ScanStatus.description:
Lifecycle of an image scan row (DB `scan_status` ENUM). Mirrors the
`registry_role` mapping pattern (`sqlx::Type` + `#[sqlx(type_name = ...)]`).

ScanSummary.description:
Lightweight projection for read-views (catalog/tag badges) and list endpoints
— never carries the full `report` blob.

ScanTrigger.description:
What requested a scan (DB `registry_scan_trigger` ENUM). Mirrors the
`ScanTrigger` carried on the `RegistryScan` CRD (`hf_lib_domain_core`); kept
as a separate console-DB enum so the domain crate takes no console dep.

ScmBranch.description: A branch the user can pick after settling on a repo.

ScmRepository.description: A repository the user can pick to clone into a dev-workstation.

default_branch.description: Default branch — pre-fills the branch picker as a sensible starting point.

description.description: Short description for the picker subtitle, if the SCM has one.

full_name.description:
Canonical `owner/name` identifier — fed to the workstation as the clone
source after we prefix it with the provider's HTTPS base.

html_url.description: Browsable URL (for the "open on GitHub" link in the picker).

updated_at.description:
ISO-8601 timestamp of the last push, used to sort and label the picker
rows ("Updated 3 days ago").

SecretType.description: The type of value stored in the secret.

iam_sa_client_id.description: The client_id of the Keycloak client.

iam_sa_credentials_path.description: The secret path in the secret store for the service account credentials.

iam_sa_id.description: This is the storage ID of the Keycloak client NOT the client_id.

SessionSummary.description:
A session row for the chat session rail. `title` is whatever opencode
stored at create time — for our sessions that is the agent preset name
(e.g. "Code Helper"), so the rail reads as a list of named conversations
instead of opaque ids.

created_at.description: ISO-8601 created timestamp from opencode's `time.created` (epoch ms).

title.description: Human title set at create time; empty if opencode had none.

SetUserAttributesRequest.description: Request to set all attributes for a user (replaces existing).

attributes.description:
List of attributes in `namespace::value` format.
Example: `["region::occitanie", "department::sales"]`

flatten.description: Whether to flatten directory structure (true) or preserve it (false)

length.description: Number of characters from SHA256 hash to use as suffix

name.description: InferenceService name

TagResponse.description: One tag with its manifest digest and best-effort size.

digest.description:
The manifest content digest (`Docker-Content-Digest`). Empty if the
registry did not return one.

size_bytes.description:
Best-effort manifest size in bytes (`config.size + Σ layers[].size`).
`0` for a multi-arch index/manifest-list (no config/layers; recursion to
sub-manifests is deferred — best-effort, marked approximate in the usage
view's `byte_usage_pending`).

TagsResponse.description: Tags for a repository under a project the caller may pull from.

code.description: Stable identifier the frontend can branch on.

message.description: Human-readable detail, mostly for surfacing in the UI.

registry.description: Registry host the request was made against.

tag.description: Tag (or digest) portion of the reference.

image_pull_secret.description: Optional secret-manager entry name with OCI credentials. Omit for public images.

TestImagePullResponse.description:
Result of an interactive image-pull credential test.

The endpoint always returns `200 OK` on a completed test — `ok` discriminates
between a successful manifest probe and a structured registry-side failure.
Surfacing failures as part of a single response shape (rather than a 4xx with
a different body) keeps the generated OpenAPI client typed and lets the UI
branch on `reason` without piggy-backing on HTTP status codes.

ok.description:
`true` when the registry returned a manifest, `false` for any
structured pull failure (auth, not found, tag not found, network, …).

resolved_digest.description:
Manifest digest reported by the registry, e.g. `sha256:…`. Present when
`ok` is `true`.

ok.description:
`true` when the registry validated the credentials or accepted the
request anonymously; `false` for any structured login failure.

dc_iceberg_id.description: UUID of the Iceberg catalog Data Container

dd_trino_int.description: UUID of the Trino Data Dock

model_base_url.description: Base URL for the model API (e.g., vLLM endpoint). Defaults to Mistral API when not set.

tables.description:
Allowlist applied when `exposure_mode` is `selected`; ignored (and
cleared) when `all`.

UpdateContextProviderBody.description: Request to update a context provider.

UpdateContextualRestrictionBody.description: Request to update a contextual restriction.

UpdateDataDockSecuritySettingsBody.description: Request to update datadock security settings.

UpdateDevWorkstationRequest.description:
Partial update of a workstation's inference settings. Every field is
optional; only present fields are applied to the CR `spec.inference`.

inference_model.description:
Provider-qualified opencode model id to default to. When present (even
empty), replaces the workstation's default model; an empty string clears
it so the entrypoint's curated default applies again.

inference_provider.description:
Inference provider: `sapience` (in-cluster LLMaaS) or `openrouter`
(external). When present, replaces the workstation's provider.

openrouter_key_secret_id.description:
Id of a Passe Partout secret of type `plaintext` holding the OpenRouter
API key. When present, replaces the workstation's key secret reference.

UpdateOrgSecuritySettingsBody.description: Request to update organization security settings.

suspend.description: When true the underlying CronJob is suspended (no new runs will be scheduled).

description.description: `null` clears the description; absent keeps the current value.

UpdateTableClassificationBody.description: Request to update a table classification.

UpdateWorkstationTemplateRequest.description:
Request body for updating an existing workstation template.
All fields are optional: absent fields leave the existing value unchanged.

script.description:
Send an empty string `""` to clear the explicit script and fall back to
the tooling-generated one. Absent = do not change the script.

tooling.description:
Replaces the tooling list. When provided (with no `script`), triggers
script regeneration from the new tooling.

UserAttributesEntry.description: Entry for a single user's attributes.

attribute_sources.description: Maps attribute names to the provider name that supplied them.

VettedBaseResponse.description: A single vetted base image entry returned by the base-picker endpoint.

VulnerabilityCounts.description:
Per-severity finding counts for one scanned digest. v1 is informational only
(the blocking quality gate is a v2.x follow-up).

WarningSeverity.description:
Severity shapes the UI's visual treatment: `danger` is red (Argo/Flux
resurrection), `warning` is amber (pipeline resurrection, cascading
drops), `info` is blue (child counts, row estimates).

WorkstationFile.description:
One repo file surfaced by the read-only file finder. `path` is repo-relative
(the shape opencode's `/find/file` returns); `name` is the basename, derived
here so the frontend doesn't have to split paths.

name.description: Basename, e.g. `main.rs`.

WorkstationTemplateResponse.description: A workstation template as returned by the API.

baseImage.description: Base image the workstation pod will run on.

name.description: Kubernetes CRD name — the stable identifier for subsequent API calls.

setupScript.description:
The resolved setup script that the operator runs at provisioning.
`null` when neither tooling nor an explicit script is declared.

WorkstationTemplateScopeDto.description: Visibility scope of a workstation template.

name.description: Package name as recognised by the package manager.

base_hash.description:
Hash the client loaded. The write is rejected with 409 if the on-disk file
has diverged since (the agent or terminal edited it). Omit to force-write.

hash.description:
sha256 of the freshly written bytes — the client adopts it as its new
precondition token.
